title: raukaua × parvus
intro: Raukaua × parvus, also known as the hybrid of Raukaua laetevirens and Raukaua parvus, is a type of plant native to New Zealand, which is a member of the ginger family and is commonly known as harakeke. The leaves and roots of the raukaua × parvus plant are traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of raukaua × parvus include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of colds and flu. The herbal preparations of raukaua × parvus include infusions made from its leaves and roots, as well as tinctures and salves that can be applied topically.
